description:
Workflow pipelines can have gate phases that require approval before proceeding (e.g., human review gates between implementation and merge). The CLI has `workflow phase approve` but there is no MCP equivalent. This means MCP clients cannot approve gates, blocking workflow progress for daemon-managed workflows.

## Fix
1. Add `ao.workflow.phase.approve` MCP tool.
2. Input: `{ workflow_id: String, phase_id: Option<String>, feedback: Option<String>, project_root: Option<String> }`.
3. Maps to CLI: `workflow phase approve --id <workflow_id> [--phase-id <phase_id>] [--feedback <feedback>]`.
4. Returns the updated workflow state after approval.

## Key Files
- `crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/operations/ops_mcp.rs` — new tool definition
- `crates/orchestrator-cli/src/cli_types/workflow_types.rs` — existing WorkflowPhaseCommand::Approve
title: Add ao.workflow.phase.approve MCP tool for gate approval
